brandVoice.storyline: We've already met — online, on time.
brandVoice.audience: Maintenance engineers, procurement, and technical buyers at industrial OEMs and production plants. B2B. They already know what they need; they want to order it without friction.
messagingPillars: Speed & availability: in-stock items 48/72h across Europe
Ease: DirectCUT, DirectUP, 3D files, track & trace, no small-order surcharge
Human support: 1:1 live chat & email in 7 languages
signaturePhrases: We've already met – online, on time.
Our standard components are ready when you are.
dos: Use bullet points and tables for scannability
Translate technical data into user value
donts: No CAD drawings or schematic visuals
No generic stock photos (people in suits)

NEVER claim direct integration with SAP Ariba, Coupa, Jaggaer, or any e-procurement / ERP system. APSOparts has NO direct ERP/e-procurement connector. The only related feature is DirectUP — customers upload a PDF order (generated by their own ERP / procurement tool) and DirectUP parses it into an APSOparts cart. Always describe it as 'upload your PDF order' or 'DirectUP imports your purchase order PDF', never 'connects to' or 'integrates with' a procurement system

Do not invent product features, certifications, or capabilities. If unsure, omit the claim. Verified APSOparts shop features: DirectUP (PDF order upload), DirectCUT (cut-to-size configurator for plastics), Quickorder (CSV part-list re-ordering), real-time stock + lead-time visibility, 48/72h delivery across Europe, no small-order surcharge, 1:1 live chat & email in 7 languages

positioningGuard.apsoparts: Standard components, fast transactional e-commerce, 100K+ products, 48/72h EU delivery
positioningGuard.angstPfisterParent: Custom engineering solutions, bespoke design, project work
positioningGuard.rule: APSOparts content must never cross into custom/design/bespoke engineering territory. Keep the brand in the standard-component transactional lane. When a customer need sounds like custom work, refer to the parent (Angst+Pfister) rather than quoting custom capability on APSOparts channels.

pageStructure: Selection Guidance ('Choose this if / Consider alternatives if')
Downloads (supporting only — never for key info)
styleRules: Write for decision-making, not just data delivery
Scannable layout — bullets, tables, short chunks
SI units mandatory (°C, Shore A, mm, bar)

postTemplate: Reinforce ease of replacement / remove friction
socialMediaRules.lengthGuidance: Short paragraphs, mobile-first. Capture attention within the first second.
visualRules: Realistic environments (hands + tools + components in context)
Avoid: white-background isolated product shots, CAD, stock suits, promotional badges

internalSearchTrends.signal: 6× YoY spike (158 → 991 sessions Mar 2026). Priority opportunity.
High sustained volume, strong commercial intent
German-language hose demand — external EN keyword list misses this

keywordSignals.externalListGaps: External SEO list is English and generic (pods, hoses, brackets — ambiguous). Internal Magento search is German technical codes (ffkm, epdm, moosgummi). The SEO list does not reflect actual customer demand.

categoryIntelligence.contentGap: 304 leaf categories (74%) have no SEO category text. Primary first-workflow opportunity for the hub.

imageDNA.purpose: Visual consistency for APSOparts content across external digital channels and shop mood images. Adapts the Angst+Pfister group visual language for cross-brand recognition (parent: bolder hero, custom-engineering studio look; APSOparts: same family, but lighter, more transactional, peer-to-peer with maintenance/procurement).
imageDNA.lighting: Soft natural daylight from a window or overhead diffused. Avoid harsh studio rim-light or saturated marketing rendering. Subtle warm shadows are fine; never neon or HDR.
imageDNA.palette: Industrial neutrals (steel grey, brushed metal, raw concrete, oiled wood). APSO red (#ed1b2f) only as a small accent — a tool handle, a brand mark, a thread of light. Never red as the dominant color of a scene.
imageDNA.framing: Eye-level or slightly top-down workbench angles. Hands-and-tools-in-context shots are the signature. Wide-establishing plant overviews are fine for plant-manager content. Never centered, symmetrical hero compositions.
imageDNA.depthOfField: Real photographic DoF. Background slightly out of focus, subject in focus. No tilt-shift, no fake bokeh.
imageDNA.anpFamilyRule: Cross-brand recognition: same lighting language and palette as Angst+Pfister parent. Difference is subject — parent shows custom solutions / engineered prototypes, APSOparts shows standard components in honest use.

sceneRules: Match the operation to the right tool. Cutting plastic stock = CNC mill, bandsaw, or precision saw — NEVER a hand cutter or utility knife. Replacing an O-ring = gloved hand + correct pick tool — NEVER pliers. Tightening a fitting = correct wrench size — NEVER adjustable spanner on a precision part.
Show realistic component sizes. An O-ring on a fingertip is fine; an O-ring being lifted with two hands is not.

Components must be installed/handled in plausible context. PEEK rod stock belongs near a CNC, not a workbench with a knife. Hydraulic seals belong in a hydraulic cylinder cross-section, not floating on white.
Hands and gloves should match the operation. Bare hands for clean assembly of small seals; nitrile gloves for chemical/food/pharma; leather work gloves for heavy hardware.
Wear and weathering are honest. Tools and benches can show real use. People should not look like models.
No impossible physics: no liquids defying gravity, no parts hovering, no perfectly aligned identical parts in unrealistic patterns.
hardNo: No CAD renders, exploded views, schematics, blueprint overlays.
No white-background isolated product shots (those belong on the PDP, not in mood imagery).
No stock-photo people in business suits.
No fear or catastrophe imagery (broken parts, leaks framed as drama, alarm-red overlays).
No promotional badges, price tags, percentage signs, sale stickers.
No HR/team-building shots (handshakes, conference rooms, smiling team huddles).
No futuristic sci-fi (chrome robots, neon, hologram UIs) unless explicitly the automation/sensors brief.
No watermarks, no logos overlaid, no text rendered into the image.

audienceSceneHints.Maintenance engineers: industrial workshop, hands with correct tool (right spanner, pick), components on a worn-but-clean workbench
audienceSceneHints.Procurement / buyers: office-in-plant setting, parts boxes or catalogue pages, laptop showing shop UI
audienceSceneHints.Design engineers: engineering office, physical prototype samples in hand, clean workshop adjacent
audienceSceneHints.R&D engineers: R&D lab, test rig, gloved hand inspecting a component under measurement
audienceSceneHints.Plant managers: production floor overview, elevated angle over machinery, safety gear on a nearby operator
audienceSceneHints.Production / operations: live production line, machine in motion, operator in context
audienceSceneHints.Quality assurance: QA bench, calipers/gauges, precision measurement of a part
audienceSceneHints.Automation engineers: industrial automation skid, sensors and cables, control cabinet in background
audienceSceneHints.Facility managers: building plant room, pipework, valves and facility infrastructure
audienceSceneHints.MRO / aftermarket technicians: field service context, service van or toolbox, technician swapping a part on site
audienceSceneHints.OEM product managers: OEM assembly line, subassembly in focus, premium industrial aesthetic
audienceSceneHints.Food & beverage engineers: food-grade clean stainless environment, CIP piping, sanitary seals/fittings
audienceSceneHints.Chemical process engineers: chemical plant, stainless reactor or piping, chemical-resistant seals/gaskets
audienceSceneHints.Pharma / GMP engineers: GMP clean room, white surfaces, sanitary components, operator in clean garments
audienceSceneHints.Hydraulics & pneumatics specialists: hydraulic power unit, pneumatic cylinders, pressure gauges visible
audienceSceneHints.Distributors / resellers: warehouse aisle, neutral B2B logistics, packaged components on racks
audienceSceneHints.C-level / technical directors: wider plant overview, strategic calm composition

categorySceneHints.Sealing Technology: O-rings, gaskets and seals shown in real context (on a flange, hydraulic cylinder, or rotating shaft). Cross-section vignettes acceptable if they look like real cuts, not CAD.
categorySceneHints.Engineering Plastics Technology: PEEK / POM / PTFE plastic stock and machined parts shown next to a CNC mill, bandsaw or precision lathe — never a hand cutter on plastic stock.
categorySceneHints.Fluid Handling Technology: hoses, couplings, clamps, check valves on a real fluid circuit. Pressure gauge or test bench in frame is a plus.
categorySceneHints.Antivibration Technology: vibration mounts, bushings, buffers installed on a machine frame or motor mount.
categorySceneHints.Drive Technology: timing belts, pulleys, couplings, brackets on a real drive train.
categorySceneHints.Sensors and Power: industrial sensors, cables and control components wired into a machine.

midjourneyTranslation.note: Midjourney prompt patterns from the existing APSO image library. Translate intent (not literal syntax) into Gemini Nano Banana Pro prompts, which prefer descriptive sentences over comma-stacked tags.

patterns.mj: industrial workshop, hands working, soft natural light, shallow depth of field, 35mm, --ar 16:9 --style raw
patterns.gemini: Photorealistic editorial photograph of an industrial workshop. Hands in the foreground working on a component with the correct tool. Soft natural light from a side window. Shallow depth of field — subject sharp, background gently out of focus. 35mm-like perspective. Honest, peer-to-peer industrial mood, not glossy stock.

patterns.mj: hydraulic cylinder cutaway, EPDM seal install, macro detail, studio softbox, --ar 1:1
patterns.gemini: Realistic close-up macro photograph of a hydraulic cylinder with an EPDM seal being installed in its groove using the correct pick tool. Soft, even diffused light (no studio rim glare). Sharp subject in focus, the cylinder body fading out of focus. Square framing. No CAD or schematic look.

patterns.mj: GMP clean room, sanitary piping, operator in clean suit, soft daylight, --ar 16:9
patterns.gemini: Realistic photograph of a GMP clean room with sanitary stainless piping. An operator in a clean garment is checking a sanitary seal. Soft, neutral daylight. White surfaces, no neon or color tint. Calm composition, eye-level perspective. No futuristic sci-fi styling.

goldImageReferences.title: PEEK / POM rod stock at machining station (training reference 2026-04)
goldImageReferences.notes: Plastic engineering rods staged on a brushed-steel workbench near a CNC. This is the correct way to show machinable plastics. NOT with a utility knife.

personas.name: Markus Weber
personas.description: Risk-averse strategic purchaser running corporate procurement at a mid-large German-speaking machine builder.
personas.internalNotes: country IN ('DE','CH','AT') AND apic_ap STARTS_WITH '1.1' AND (job_position='Purchaser' OR jobtitle CONTAINS einkauf|leiter einkauf). Population: ~1,000 companies, ~1,400 contacts. Revenue 2025: ~CHF 1.8M.
personas.roles: Strategischer Einkäufer / Leiter Einkauf at a 50-500 FTE machine-building OEM. Owns supplier consolidation, frame agreements, ERP / e-procurement integration, approved-vendor list. Reports to COO.
personas.goals: Find a reliable, certified second source that fits the existing e-procurement flow without re-spec'ing approved parts. Vendor consolidation, predictable lead time, price stability, frame agreements, compliance docs on demand.
personas.challenges: Approved-vendor list slow to change. Entrenched incumbents with real switching cost. Risk-averse — needs references, audit history, samples. ERP/EDI integration is a hard requirement.

demographics.education: Diplom-Ingenieur / Bachelor in engineering or business, often dual-track
demographics.location: DACH industrial belt — Baden-Württemberg, Bavaria, Swiss Mittelland, Vorarlberg

personas.story: Markus has spent 15 years inside the same machine-building OEM, working his way from operative buyer to Leiter Einkauf. He runs SAP Ariba, manages a 2-person team, reports to the COO. Annual KPIs: supplier reduction (-10% per year) and OTIF >95%. He distrusts marketing pitches and only opens emails from suppliers who passed his audit. He'll meet at Hannover Messe, listen carefully, and ask for a sample plus three reference customers before agreeing to the next step. Winning Markus takes six months; once won, the relationship lasts a decade.

personas.name: Sophie Müller
personas.description: Online-first Gen-Y purchaser running procurement on tools, not phone calls.
personas.internalNotes: apso_customer IN ('APSOgrowth','Growth Engine Customer') AND (job_position='Purchaser' OR jobtitle CONTAINS einkauf|buyer|acheteur|acquisti). Population: ~836 companies, ~1,267 contacts. Revenue 2025: ~CHF 3.5M.
personas.roles: Operative Einkäuferin / digital procurement lead at a 30-150 FTE growth-stage SME. Often the only buyer at the company. Reports directly to operations or finance.
personas.goals: Get the right part today with full price + lead-time transparency. End-to-end self-service, saved carts, recurring orders, account pricing, mobile-friendly experience, automated tracking.
personas.challenges: Internal procurement still on paper POs and manual approval flows. Delivery time outside DE/CH on niche items. Trust threshold for online ordering >CHF 5,000.

demographics.education: Bachelor in business administration or supply-chain management
demographics.location: Cross-DACH and EU Tier-2 cities, hybrid working

personas.story: Sophie joined her current employer three years ago as the first dedicated procurement hire. Half her week is on Slack and the e-shop, the other half firefighting late deliveries from incumbents. She found APSOparts via Google when she searched for a specific part number a colleague had bookmarked. She placed her first order on her phone, in the warehouse, with a coffee in her other hand. She's open to switching suppliers if the digital experience is better — already done it once with another category.

personas.name: Pietro Rossi
personas.description: Northern-Italian procurement lead who builds long relationships and expects an Italian-speaking partner.
personas.internalNotes: country='Italy' OR hs_country_code='IT' OR hs_language='it'. Population: ~4,133 companies, ~5,243 contacts (12% of base — completely missing from previous deck). Revenue 2025: ~CHF 1.2M.
personas.roles: Responsabile Acquisti or Ufficio Acquisti at a 20-200 FTE Italian SME — manufacturing, food/beverage, pharma. Often reports directly to Titolare.
personas.goals: Build a relationship with a partner he can call directly, in Italian, that ships quickly to Northern Italy. Italian-language e-shop, datasheets, support, invoicing. References from Italian Pharma / Food / Mechanics customers.
personas.challenges: Trust threshold higher with foreign suppliers — face-to-face is non-negotiable for the first deal. Italian competitors entrenched (PIANCONI, Sealmaster). Payment-term expectations 60-90 days vs DACH 30. English-only assets a non-starter.

demographics.education: Diploma di perito tecnico or Laurea in Ingegneria; often family-business background

personas.story: Pietro has been at the same family-run mechanical-components SME for 12 years, hired straight out of his perito industriale qualification. He rarely leaves Italy except for MECSPE (Parma) and A&T (Turin). He'd rather hear 'Salve Pietro, sono Marco da APSO' on the phone than open a marketing email. When he changes supplier — twice a decade — it's because his cousin at another factory recommended them. Localised content, an Italian sales rep, and stock in Northern Italy turn him from 'diffidente' into a long-term advocate.

personas.name: Andreas Schmidt
personas.description: Owner-manager of a small DACH SME — relationship-led, time-poor, sceptical of digital channels.
personas.internalNotes: (job_position IN ('Managing Director/CEO','Owner') OR jobtitle CONTAINS geschäftsführer|inhaber|owner|gf|ceo) AND country IN ('DE','CH','AT'). Population: ~1,949 companies, ~2,026 contacts. Revenue 2025: ~CHF 770K.
personas.roles: Geschäftsführer / Inhaber of a 1-50 FTE family-owned manufacturing or service business. Often founder or 2nd-generation. Wears every hat — sales, finance, sometimes operating the machines himself.
personas.goals: Don't waste my time. One trustworthy partner, fair prices, one named contact across all orders. Loyalty pricing, long-term frame agreements, on-call technical support.
personas.challenges: Highly loyal to incumbents — switching is rare. Time-poor — won't engage with long content or onboarding. Sceptical of digital channels and marketing automation. Price decisive only when relationships are equal.

demographics.education: Mix of Meister, Berufsausbildung + part-time business school, or hands-on family-business training
demographics.location: DACH rural and Mittelstand — small towns, industrial estates outside major cities

personas.story: Andreas inherited his father's 25-person component shop fifteen years ago. He still walks the workshop floor every morning, knows every customer by first name, and signs every PO over CHF 5,000. The fastest way to lose his business is to send him a marketing email; the fastest way to win it is to show up at his Rotary lunch with a hand-signed letter from a senior executive. He buys from suppliers his father bought from, and when he changes — usually after a delivery failure — the new one tends to keep his business for the next decade.

personas.name: Luca Liebertz
personas.description: Cut-to-size workshop owner who has to win quotes in 48 hours on commodity-priced plastics.
personas.internalNotes: apic_ap = '4.1 Processing Companies, Toll Manufact.'. Population: 4,028 companies, 6,138 contacts. Revenue 2025: CHF 2.35M.
personas.roles: Inhaber, Werkstattleiter, AVOR, or Chef d'atelier of a 5-50 FTE turning-and-milling workshop, plastic processor, or seal manufacturer. Often family-run.
personas.goals: Win the end-customer offer in 48 hours by quoting the cheapest available semi-finished material in the size required. DirectCut configurator, real-time stock + lead-time, no MOQ pain on commodities, food-grade certified material with documentation in one click.
personas.challenges: Heavy price competition — margin-thin commodity plastics. MOQs hurt on niche materials. Older workshops with low tech-savviness — UX must be simple. Order changes after submission a friction point.

demographics.education: Mechanical Berufsausbildung + Meisterbrief; in IT/FR equivalent diploma di perito or BTS productique
demographics.location: DACH + Northern Italy + Eastern France industrial pockets

personas.story: Luca took over his father's plastics workshop ten years ago and now runs it with five employees. His phone rings with a customer RFQ at 9 a.m.; by 5 p.m. he must have a quote out, knowing his customer is calling three of his competitors at the same time. The deciding factor is rarely the workshop's craft — it's whether the raw material is in stock and shipping today at a price that leaves him 8% margin. APSOparts' DirectCut feature is the difference between sending a quote in 30 minutes versus losing the deal because the raw-material supplier didn't reply on time.

personas.name: Tomas Becker
personas.description: Plant-floor maintenance lead who buys when something breaks — speed, not price, decides.
personas.internalNotes: apic_ap LIKE '2.%' OR job_position='Maintenance' OR jobtitle CONTAINS mechaniker|wartung|maintenance|manutenzione. Population: 3,524 companies, 6,221 contacts. Revenue 2025: CHF 1.71M.
personas.roles: Maintenance technician / Mechaniker / Betriebsmechaniker / Responsable maintenance / Manutentore at a manufacturing, food, pharma, or public-services plant. Reports to plant manager. Buys when production breaks.
personas.goals: Drop-in replacement, in stock, shipped today — line back up before the next shift. Same-day or next-day delivery. Cross-reference search (competitor part → APSOparts equivalent). Mobile-friendly stock view. Sales rep on the phone NOW.
personas.challenges: Competitor parts already in machines — switching means re-engineering. Procurement approval delays during downtime are unacceptable. Need exact-match certifications (FDA, ATEX, food contact). International delivery time can lose the order.

demographics.education: Mechanical or electrical Berufsausbildung; often Meister or Industriemeister
demographics.location: Manufacturing belts across DACH and EU — automotive corridors, food clusters, pharma hubs

personas.story: Tomas works the early shift at a packaging plant. At 4:47 a.m. one Monday a conveyor seal failed and the line went down. By 5:30 a.m. he had searched APSOparts on his tablet (covered in machine grease), found the food-grade equivalent of the OEM part, called the +41 number, paid by company card, and had the part delivered before lunch. He doesn't read marketing emails. He doesn't attend webinars. But he saves the supplier who got him out of trouble at 5 a.m., and tells the next plant he visits.

personas.name: Jan Hoffmann
personas.description: OEM design engineer who specs-in parts at the drawing stage — never signs the PO but always blocks it.
personas.internalNotes: hs_role='engineering' OR job_position IN ('Engineer','Project Leader') OR jobtitle CONTAINS konstrukteur|projektleiter|engineer|techniker|ingegnere. Population: ~3,004 companies, ~3,882 contacts. Revenue 2025: CHF 2.7M (lifetime value much higher — spec-in compounds for 10+ years).
personas.roles: Konstrukteur / Projektleiter / Entwicklungsingenieur / R&D Engineer at an OEM (machine builder, pharma, pumps & valves, semicon, aviation). Owns the design specification.
personas.goals: Find the right material spec for the application without spending a week reading datasheets. Application-engineer access for spec reviews. Sample programme. Configurable / engineered solutions. Searchable certification library (FDA, EC1935, ATEX).
personas.challenges: Will dismiss any 'too-marketing' content. Loyal to brands once spec'd in. Rarely engages in commercial conversations — passes the baton to procurement. Will reject a supplier outright if the datasheet doesn't match field-test results.

demographics.education: Diplom-Ingenieur or M.Sc. in mechanical, materials, or chemical engineering; FH/HES or Fachhochschule background
demographics.location: OEM R&D centres — Stuttgart, Munich, Zurich, Linz, Milan, Eindhoven

personas.story: Jan spends most of his time in CAD and FEA, with the rest in technical meetings and prototype labs. Suppliers who send him glossy brochures get filtered. Suppliers who send him an application note with FEA results, three reference customers in his industry, and a sample within five days get a meeting. Once he writes the supplier's part number into the BOM, that supplier owns the line for the lifetime of the product — which in industrial machinery is 10-15 years. A sale to Jan looks small at first; it compounds for a decade.

personas.name: Reseller Partner
personas.description: Authorised channel partner who resells APSOparts in their territory, balancing margin against end-customer service.
personas.internalNotes: apic_ap IN ('3.1 Certified Partner','3.2 Authorized Reseller','3.3 Reseller'). Population: 1,052 companies, ~2,611 contacts. Revenue 2025: CHF 3.5M (channel-attributed).
personas.roles: Geschäftsführer or Country Manager of an industrial distributor / regional dealer / value-added reseller (5-200 FTE). Holds the relationship with end-customers and decides which suppliers to push.
personas.goals: Win the end-customer with a competitive partner price + reliable supply, while keeping enough margin to fund their own sales effort. Tiered partner programme, co-funded marketing (MDF), priority technical support, lead-sharing pipeline.
personas.challenges: Channel conflict if APSOparts sells direct in their territory at a lower price. Margin pressure forces them to shop alternatives. Onboarding cost — partners need product training. Lead-flow disputes when attribution is unclear.

demographics.incomeRange: highly variable — sole traders to EUR 50M+ distributors
demographics.education: Business or technical degree + 10-20 years in industrial distribution
demographics.location: EU markets where APSOparts has limited direct presence — Eastern Europe, Iberia, Nordics, parts of IT/FR

personas.story: The Reseller Partner runs a 30-person distribution business that has sold seals, plastics, and fluid-handling components in their region for two decades. They manage 12 supplier brands and decide each year which ones to feature on their cover catalogue. APSOparts won them three years ago because of stock availability and technical support; they keep the relationship because of margin protection (no direct undercutting in their territory) and because a dedicated APSO partner manager flies out twice a year for joint customer visits. Their sales team is APSOparts' field force in regions APSOparts cannot cover directly.
